name: adconnect-beta
description: MCP-style tool contract for reviewed sponsored developer-tool offers shown through ChatGPT and Codex.
principles: Sponsored offers must help with the user's current developer task.
Paid placement must be disclosed and separated from organic recommendations.
Users must approve external side effects before they happen.
Advertiser claims, links, integration steps, and hosted connectors require review before activation.

tools.name: search_offers
tools.description: Find relevant sponsored and organic developer-tool offers for a user intent.

intent.description: The user's task or buying/integration intent.

category.description: Developer-tool category such as email, hosting, auth, observability, database, payments, analytics, ci_cd, or security.

constraints.description: User constraints such as budget, compliance, deployment target, scale, or company stage.

outputRequirements: Return sponsored and organic offers in separate arrays.
Include disclosure text for every sponsored offer.
Include whyShown or relevanceReasons for every sponsored offer.
Exclude offers dismissed or reported in the same session when session context is available.

tools.name: get_offer_details
tools.description: Return reviewed offer terms, docs, setup notes, eligibility, claims, pricing notes, and disclosure guidance.
outputRequirements: Include sponsored disclosure when the offer is paid.
Include eligibility, expiration, region, pricing caveats, claim requirements, and review status when available.
Include advertiser-provided claims separately from AdConnect or assistant analysis.

tools.name: compare_offers
tools.description: Compare selected sponsored and organic vendors across developer-relevant decision criteria.

criteria.description: Criteria such as pricing, limits, setup effort, docs, framework support, compliance, lock-in, or scale.

outputRequirements: Keep sponsored status visible for each offer.
Compare on user-relevant criteria, not advertiser bid amount.
Highlight missing data instead of inventing claims.

tools.name: claim_offer
tools.description: Start an approved claim, signup, credit redemption, or advertiser handoff for a sponsored offer.

userApproval.description: Must be true only after explicit user approval.

claimContext.description: Optional reviewed context needed for the claim, such as selected plan or company stage.

outputRequirements: Return the handoff URL, claim token, expiration, and next steps when available.
Return what data may be sent to the advertiser.
Return whether the claim is reversible or cancellable.

tools.name: get_integration_steps
tools.description: Return reviewed setup instructions, SDK guidance, configuration requirements, validation steps, and optional hosted connector actions.

environment.description: Local, staging, production, or hosted target.

outputRequirements: Separate informational setup steps from actions that require approval.
Include required environment variables, package names, docs links, and validation steps.
Identify any advertiser-hosted connector action and its side effects before use.

tools.name: track_event
tools.description: Record analytics and audit events for sponsored offer presentation and user actions.
outputRequirements: Return a stable event id and recorded timestamp.
Do not record events for actions that did not occur.

tools.name: report_offer
tools.description: Submit a user report for misleading, unsafe, irrelevant, expired, low-quality, or incorrectly targeted offers.
outputRequirements: Return report id, status, and whether the offer should be suppressed for the session.
Create or trigger a moderation review item.

presentationRequirements: Show sponsored offers only in a labeled sponsored section.
Include why the offer was shown.
State material eligibility, price, region, expiration, and account requirements when available.
Do not present paid placement as unbiased ranking.
Do not repeat dismissed or reported offers in the same session.
reviewRequirements: Hosted connector scopes and side effects
